name: Summer Olympics
description: Hosts, heroes, and headlines from the modern Summer Games (1896–today).

questions.q: Which city hosted the first modern Summer Olympics in 1896?

source.notes: Held in the rebuilt Panathenaic Stadium.

questions.q: Who founded the International Olympic Committee in 1894?

source.notes: French educator who revived the Games.

questions.q: Which American Indian athlete famously won both decathlon and pentathlon at the 1912 Stockholm Games?

source.notes: Stripped of medals over amateurism rules; reinstated by IOC in 1983 (then full restoration in 2022).

questions.q: Which Black American track-and-field star won 4 golds at the 1936 Berlin Olympics, embarrassing Hitler?
Which 1936 Berlin Games innovation became a fixture of every modern Olympics?

source.notes: Carl Diem proposed the relay from Olympia, Greece to the host city.

questions.q: At which Games was the modern marathon distance of 26.2 miles (42.195 km) standardized?

source.notes: Length set so the race could start at Windsor Castle and end in front of the royal box.

questions.q: Which 1972 Olympics were marred by a terrorist attack that killed 11 Israeli athletes and coaches?
Which U.S. swimmer won 7 gold medals at the 1972 Munich Olympics — a record for one Games until Phelps broke it in 2008?

source.notes: Phelps later won 8 golds at Beijing 2008.

questions.q: How many gold medals did Michael Phelps win at the 2008 Beijing Olympics?
Which Jamaican sprinter is the only athlete to win the 100 m and 200 m at three consecutive Olympics (2008, 2012, 2016)?

source.notes: The 'triple-triple' (the relay golds were later affected by a teammate's DQ).

questions.q: Which U.S. high jumper introduced the back-first 'Flop' technique at the 1968 Mexico City Olympics?

source.notes: Won gold and revolutionized the event.

questions.q: Which boxer, then known as Cassius Clay, won Olympic light-heavyweight gold in 1960?

source.notes: Rome 1960. Clay changed his name to Ali in 1964.

questions.q: Which Asian city was the first to host the Summer Olympics, in 1964?
Which 2016 Olympics were the first held in South America?
Which city was the first to host the Summer Olympics three times (in 2012, after 1908 and 1948)?
Which U.S. city hosted the 1996 Summer Olympics — known for the Centennial Games?
Which two countries' boycotts (1980 and 1984) crippled consecutive Olympics?

source.notes: USA boycotted Moscow 1980 over Afghanistan; USSR retaliated by boycotting LA 1984.

questions.q: Which gymnast scored the first perfect 10 in Olympic history at the 1976 Montreal Games?

source.notes: Romanian gymnast, age 14. Scoreboard couldn't display 10.0 — flashed 1.00.

questions.q: Which city is scheduled to host the 2028 Summer Olympics?
Which Australian city will host the 2032 Summer Olympics?
